key_insight: OptiLoop shows that LLM-generated optimization agents can look correct in isolation yet fail semantically during coordination, so verification must happen inside the interaction loop.
engineering_relevance: This matters for decentralized optimization systems where privacy, incentives, and constraints must be respected across agents, not just within a single local solver.
method: Generate local optimization agents from natural-language specs
Run short bounded ADMM-style coordination checks against a fixed reference counterparty
Use structured behavioral/static evidence to trigger code repair or corrected-formulation repair
evidence_signal: On 40 held-out scenarios, OptiLoop-Full improved objective match from 66.0% to 93.0% and social match from 68.5% to 89.0%, while reducing mean objective gap from 15.3% to 3.5% and mean social gap from 7.6% to 2.0%.
watch_item: The approach relies on a fixed reference counterparty and bounded coordination runs, so performance may depend on how well those checks approximate real multi-party deployments.
paper_title: OptiLoop: Coordination-in-the-Loop Verification and Repair for LLM-Generated Optimization Agents
